data.body:
=== EXPERIMENT RESULT (round 3): criteria reconciliation + substance + agentic tier → storage/chunking design ===

Addresses Joe's challenge: (1) verify our criteria set + that experiments used substantive not bare-bones
criteria; (2) confirm single-turn vs agentic (tool-use); (3) >12 criteria; (4) model+size-aware chunk-size
defaults; (5) facet-based criteria storage for advisory grouping that scales/stays fresh. Two new experiments
(Sonnet, +real codebase tools, ≈$5). Design committed at
docs/experiments/plan-review-criteria-storage-and-chunking.md; DSO-grounded criterion specs at
docs/experiments/data/dso-criteria-specs.md; data/harnesses under docs/experiments/data/ (criteria_v2.json,
exp1_substance.*, exp2_agentic.*, analyze4.py). Epic 5fd2 description updated with a "## Experiment-grounded
defaults" section + the deferred-defaults AC checked (still passes check_ac + clarity 7).

RECONCILIATION (Joe was right on all counts):
- My round 1-2 experiments tested a THIN, SINGLE-TURN-ONLY subset (~12) with BARE-BONES one-liners. The real v4
  §5 registry spans FOUR EXEC TIERS and is larger: DET (P1-P7), SINGLE-TURN (F1,F4,E2,E5,G5 + 2-STEP E1,E3,E6),
  AGENT/tool-using (E4,G1G2,A1,T1,T6,T7,T8), triggered overlays (T5a-e + others). Mined DSO for the FULL
  specs (real binary checklists + severity + anti-FP, cited file:line) → criteria_v2.json (12 single-turn) +
  dso-criteria-specs.md (all tiers incl agentic + overlays).

THREE CORRECTIONS TO EARLIER ROUNDS:
1. EXEC TIER is the PRIMARY axis, not chunk size. DET=parallel code (~free). SINGLE-TURN=chunkable (~$0.005-0.008
   /criterion cached). AGENT=one tool-loop per criterion, ~$0.12 + ~50s, ~15 tool calls (≈85× single-turn) —
   NOT chunkable. So chunking applies ONLY within the single-turn tier; agentic = 1-agent-each, gated by
   proportionate scrutiny.
2. "COARSE CHUNKS ARE CHEAPER" was a NO-CACHING artifact. With prompt caching (plan read at 0.1×), the cost gap
   between chunk sizes collapses 2.6×→1.55× (N=2 $0.090 vs N=12 $0.058/review). Fine chunking is NOT expensive →
   within single-turn, chunk for RELIABILITY not cost. Also narrows Opus's one-pass edge: cached fine-chunk Sonnet
   ($0.07-0.09) ≈ one Opus pass ($0.10).
3. SUBSTANCE doesn't lower the reliable chunk size — fully-specified criteria are MORE consistent at coarse N than
   one-liners (Jaccard N=4 0.61 vs 0.53; N=12 0.65 vs 0.59). Explicit checklists cut reviewer variance. Round-1/2
   chunk-size guidance is safe.

EXP2 AGENTIC (the single-turn-vs-agent answer, with REAL tools over rebar+DSO repos): E4/G1G2/A1 each ~15 tool
calls / ~9 iters / ~50s / ~$0.12. With tools they flip AMBIGUOUS→grounded — G1G2 caught our OWN epic's "solidlsp
(already in repo)" claim is FALSE (glob finds zero; solidlsp is a Serena dep, not committed). Real dogfood win the
no-tool runs could only mark AMBIGUOUS.

DESIGN DELIVERED:
- STORAGE: criterion descriptor gains exec(tier) + facet(concern/context — the advisory grouping key) +
  context_needs + routing + trigger. Generic chunker: filter→bucket by tier→pack same-facet single-turn criteria
  to chunk size→1 agent per AGENT criterion→DET as code. Adding/tuning a criterion only declares exec+facet (no
  hand-maintained chunk lists) — grouping advisory (re-packs at any chunk size). Facets are the CONCERN not
  arbitrary (Joe's instinct): ac-text-quality{F1,E2,E6}, scope-intent{F4,E3,G5}, coherence{E1}, testing{E5},
  codebase-grounding{E4,G1G2,A1}, overlay-* per concern.
- CHUNK SIZE FORMULA (single-turn tier): base_chunk{opus 12, sonnet 6 coherent / 3 plain, haiku/local 3} ×
  size_factor{trivial/moderate 1.0, large|epic|has_children 0.5}, clamp[2,n]. → Opus all-in-one cached call;
  Sonnet 6(coherent)/3(large); never full set on largest plans.
- BUDGET: cap = $0.15 base + n_agentic×$0.25 + headroom (small ~$0.15-0.40; heavy epic ~$1.5-2.0+minutes). Enable
  caching. INDETERMINATE sheds AGENT/overlay first (85× cost), never the cheap base.

NEXT: fold schema (exec/facet) + formula + per-tier budget into plan-of-record §5-§6; decompose into impl wave;
file F1-F12. Optional validation: non-DSO corpora; a weaker/local model's stable-N; tighter agentic loop / Haiku
sub-agent to lower the $0.12 agentic floor.
